No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a developer migrating from Git to Jujutsu (jj). You have an existing local Git repository at `/home/user/my-project`. Your task is to start using jj with this repository without breaking Git compatibility.

1. Navigate to `/home/user/my-project`.
2. Initialize a colocated jj repository so that jj and Git can be used together.
3. Create a new bookmark named `feature-x`.
4. Create a new file named `feature.txt` containing the text `Hello jj`.
5. Create a new jj commit (or describe the working copy) with the description `Add feature.txt`.

When finished, the repository should be a valid colocated jj/Git repo, the `feature-x` bookmark should exist and be exported to Git, and the commit should have the specified description and file.
I'll work through this step-by-step, migrating the Git repository to use Jujutsu (jj) in colocated mode.

parts.text: **Step 1 & 2: Initialize a colocated jj repository.** The `--colocate` flag (or running `jj git init --colocate` inside an existing Git repo) sets up jj to share the `.git` directory, so both tools work together.

output.output:
Done importing changes from the underlying Git repo.
Initialized repo in "."
Hint: Running `git clean -xdf` will remove `.jj/`!

parts.text: Colocated jj repository initialized. Let me verify the `.jj` directory was created alongside `.git`:

output.output:
total 4
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 41 Mar 19 09:17 .
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 32 Mar 11 12:10 ..
drwxr-xr-x 1 root root 45 Mar 11 12:10 .git
drwxr-xr-x 4 root root 72 Mar 19 09:17 .jj
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 15 Mar 11 12:10 README.md

parts.text: Both `.git` and `.jj` directories exist — the repo is colocated.

todos.content: Check if /home/user/my-project exists and has a Git repo
Verify colocated repo, bookmark, and commit

parts.text: **Step 3: Create the `feature-x` bookmark.** Let me first check the current jj log to understand the state:

output.output:
@  uyntpwtv (no email set) 2026-03-19 09:17:41 5d317252
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  ypsoummp test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:51 main 14d944a2
│  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: The working copy change (`@`) is on top of the `main` branch. Now I'll create the `feature-x` bookmark pointing to the current working copy change:

output.output:
Warning: Target revision is empty.
Created 1 bookmarks pointing to uyntpwtv 5d317252 feature-x | (empty) (no description set)
Warning: Name and email not configured. Until configured, your commits will be created with the empty identity, and can't be pushed to remotes.
Hint: To configure, run:
  jj config set --user user.name "Some One"
  jj config set --user user.email "someone@example.com"

todos.content: Check if /home/user/my-project exists and has a Git repo
Verify colocated repo, bookmark, and commit
Check if /home/user/my-project exists and has a Git repo
Verify colocated repo, bookmark, and commit

parts.text: **Step 5: Describe the working copy with the commit message, then create a new commit on top.** In jj, the working copy is automatically a change. I'll use `jj describe` to set the message, which effectively "commits" the current state:

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: uyntpwtv b1781ca2 feature-x | Add feature.txt
Parent commit (@-)      : ypsoummp 14d944a2 main | Initial commit
Warning: Name and email not configured. Until configured, your commits will be created with the empty identity, and can't be pushed to remotes.
Hint: To configure, run:
  jj config set --user user.name "Some One"
  jj config set --user user.email "someone@example.com"

parts.text: The working copy change now has the description "Add feature.txt" and is on the `feature-x` bookmark. Now let me create a new empty working copy change on top (using `jj new`) so the described commit is finalized:

output.output:
Working copy  (@) now at: ryspuyor 56cb8b87 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : uyntpwtv b1781ca2 feature-x | Add feature.txt
Warning: Name and email not configured. Until configured, your commits will be created with the empty identity, and can't be pushed to remotes.
Hint: To configure, run:
  jj config set --user user.name "Some One"
  jj config set --user user.email "someone@example.com"

parts.text: The commit with "Add feature.txt" is now finalized, and a new empty working copy sits on top.

todos.content: Check if /home/user/my-project exists and has a Git repo
Verify colocated repo, bookmark, and commit

parts.text: **Step 6: Verify everything.** Let me check the jj log, bookmark list, Git branch export, and file contents:

output.output:
@  ryspuyor (no email set) 2026-03-19 09:20:14 56cb8b87
│  (empty) (no description set)
○  uyntpwtv (no email set) 2026-03-19 09:20:09 feature-x b1781ca2
│  Add feature.txt
○  ypsoummp test@example.com 2026-03-11 12:10:51 main 14d944a2
│  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

feature-x: uyntpwtv b1781ca2 Add feature.txt
main: ypsoummp 14d944a2 Initial commit

parts.text: All verifications pass. Let me update the todo list:

todos.content: Check if /home/user/my-project exists and has a Git repo
Verify colocated repo, bookmark, and commit

input.result:
Successfully migrated the Git repository at `/home/user/my-project` to use Jujutsu (jj) in colocated mode. Here's a summary of what was done:

1. **Initialized colocated jj repo** — `jj git init --colocate` created the `.jj` directory alongside the existing `.git` directory. Both jj and Git can now be used interchangeably on this repository.

2. **Created `feature-x` bookmark** — `jj bookmark create feature-x -r @` created the bookmark. It was automatically exported to Git as a branch (verified with `git branch -a` showing `feature-x`).

3. **Created `feature.txt`** — The file contains `Hello jj` as specified.

4. **Described the commit** — `jj describe -m "Add feature.txt"` set the commit message on the working copy change, then `jj new` finalized it by creating a new empty working copy on top.

**Verification results:**
- `jj log` shows the commit `b1781ca2` with description "Add feature.txt" containing `feature.txt`, sitting on the `feature-x` bookmark, with parent on `main`.
- `jj bookmark list` confirms both `feature-x` and `main` bookmarks exist.
- `git branch -a` confirms `feature-x` was exported as a Git branch.
- `git log --oneline --all` shows the "Add feature.txt" commit is visible from Git's perspective.
- `feature.txt` contains "Hello jj".
